title: Recurrent Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia in an HIV-infected patient: A case report
author: Nina Dimitrova Tsvetkova
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: We present a case of  Pneumocystis  pneumonia (diagnosis was based on imaging X-ray and CAT examinations, and real-time PCR) in an HIV-infected patient with relapse four months later and long lasting colonization as detected by the positive real-time PCR results and lack of clinical symptoms during the follow up of the patient after second course of treatment with trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole. The wild-type  P. jirovecii  DHPS genotype was identified in all patient samples tested.
steps_raw: We present a case of  Pneumocystis  pneumonia (diagnosis was based on imaging X-ray and CAT examinations, and real-time PCR) in an HIV-infected patient with relapse four months later and long lasting colonization as detected by the positive real-time PCR results and lack of clinical symptoms during the follow up of the patient after second course of treatment with trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole. The wild-type  P. jirovecii  DHPS genotype was identified in all patient samples tested.
keywords: Pneumocystis jirovecii; Pneumocystis pneumonia (PCP); Real-time PCR; Colonization
